calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar May-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Rufus Cole, Esq. 415-956-8800 of Rufus Cole & Associates for Petitioner Golden Gate Regional Center
Dirk Van Ausdall, Esq. 415-244-4668 for Petitioner Golden Gate Regional Center.
Jay Jambeck, Esq. 415-399-9155 of Leigh Law Group for REAL PARTY IN INTEREST MARIN COUNTY PUBLIC GUARDIAN ON BEHALF OF ERIKA S.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his views on section 393. Petitioner argues entitled to challenge the decision. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentatvie ruling as follows:

REAL PARTY IN INTEREST MARIN COUNTY PUBLIC GUARDIAN ON BEHALF OF ERIKA S.'S Motion To Transfer Venue.

Real Party in Interest Erika S.'s motion to transfer venue to Marin County is granted. This case will be transferred to Marin County Superior Court and all costs of transfer must be paid by petitioner Golden Gate Regional Center. Code of Civil Procedure 393(b) is not applicable to this case because respondents Office of Administrative Hearings and Department of Developmental Services are nominal parties and this writ petition is truly against Erika, not OAH or DDS. (Sutter Union High School District v. Superior Court (1983) 140 Cal. App. 3d. 795, 798 (the public agency whose decision was sought to be set aside by a writ of mandate petition "is a nominal party whose residence is immaterial for venue purposes"); see also Hayes v. California Department of Development Services (2006) 138 Cal. App. 4th 1523 (on a writ petition seeking to set aside a decision of the OAH, the OAH is not a necessary or indispensable party and the failure to name it as a respondent within limitations period did not warrant dismissal of the writ petition).) Because Erika is neither a public officer nor did she assume the role of a public officer, by its own terms, section 393(b) is not applicable, which makes Code of Civil Procedure 395(a) the applicable venue statute. Per section 395(a), venue is proper only in Marin County where Erika resides. Erika's request that she be awarded fees and costs per Code of Civil Procedure 396(b) is denied because GGRC's choice of venue in San Francisco was not made in bad faith due to this case have several attributes related to San Francisco.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Transfer Venue
